claim: The deaths-back-projection that produces the snapshot's total-case band reproduces Imperial Method 2, with the onset-to-death growth correction taken from the Rosello 2015 BDBV gamma and the CFR set from the US CDC prior-outbreak aggregate. The implementation is original code.
value: total_cases = deaths * (1 + r/beta)^alpha / CFR; r = ln(2)/tau; Rosello gamma alpha 4.42, beta 0.388 per day; growth correction 1.7003 at tau=14; snapshot band 1012 to 1556 at deaths 238, CFR 40 to 26
source_refs: Rosello A, et al. eLife 2015. DOI 10.7554/eLife.09015, Table 5, Isiro 2012 outbreak fit.; Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.; US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.; lovs/lovs_death_back_projection.py total_cases_from_deaths and sensitivity_grid.
public_action: Keep total_cases_from_deaths linked to this audit row. Treat the 948 to 1458 band as a reproduction on the snapshot death count, not as the Imperial reference. If the Rosello gamma, the doubling-time central, or the snapshot death count changes, update this audit row and NUMBERS_AUDIT together.

claim: The May 25 public snapshot supersedes the May 24 snapshot by fix-forward (a new dated snapshot, not an in-place re-cut) and uses the US CDC Current Situation page (25 May) as the highest valid primary on the latest date for suspected, confirmed, and deaths. The endpoint takes the higher valid primary and does not average across sources or down-revise to a lower peer.

ECDC 25 May reports 101 confirmed, 904 suspected, and 119 suspected deaths. The DRC MoH 24 May dashboard aggregate reports 854 reported cases, 112 confirmed DRC cases, and 179 registered deaths.
